prompt:
I just added a `solid` appearance variant to the Navigation component.
Did you document this variant in the playground? You almost never do.
Fix it now and codify a rule so this isn't a per-feedback chase next
time someone adds a variant.
response: I now have everything I need. The diagnosis is clear and complete. Here is the full response: --- ## Diagnosis **The playground page was already updated correctly** — `playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx` already has the `solid` variant documented with a `ComponentPreview`, a code example, and a `PropsTable` entry. This was not the miss this time. The real issue you're surfacing is the **process gap**: you had to ask to find out. The agent should have updated the playground automatically as part of landing the `solid` variant in `Navigation.tsx` — without a reminder — every time, for every component. --- ## Root Cause **EAP-082 — Adding a Variant/Prop to a DS Component Without Updating the Playground Page** The anti-pattern is catalogued, a guardrail exists (`guardrail-engineering-24`: *ALWAYS update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component*), and this has still recurred. That means the rule exists at the right level but is missing **enforcement structure** — it isn't woven into the workflow for the moment a component changes, so it gets dropped under implementation pressure. There's also a compounding issue: **EAP-008 — Documenting Recurring Fixes in Docs Instead of Promoting to Rules.** The feedback has been chased multiple times (ENG-106 occ2 in the graph is literally titled *"Did you document this variant in Playground Shell?"*). The rule is there; what's missing is the trigger that fires it at the right moment. --- ## Fix ### Part 1 — Verify the playground now (no code change needed) The playground page is already correct: **`playground/src/app/components/navigation/page.tsx`** — already contains: - ✅ `ComponentPreview` for `frosted` (default) - ✅ `ComponentPreview` for `solid` with code example - ✅ `PropsTable` row for `appearance` with both union members and correct default - ✅ `SectionHeading` description mentions both variants No code change is required to the playground page itself. ### Part 2 — Codify the rule so it fires at the right moment The guardrail exists but isn't attached to the *moment of component authoring*. The fix is to add a mandatory checklist step to the `engineering-iteration` skill (the skill that handles all component changes) so the playground page check is a **pre-condition for closing any component task**, not a doc reference to remember. **`.cursor/skills/engineering-iteration/SKILL.md`** — add this as a mandatory close-out step: ```markdown ## Mandatory Close-Out Checklist (runs before every response) Before reporting a component task as done, verify each item: - [ ] **Playground parity (EAP-082 / guardrail-engineering-24):** If you added, renamed, or removed a prop, variant, or visual state in any `src/components/ui/*/` file, open the corresponding `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` and confirm: 1. A `ComponentPreview` block exists for every variant value (one per member of the union type, not just the default). 2. The `PropsTable` entry for that prop lists every union member, the correct default, and a description. 3. The `SectionHeading` description mentions the new variant by name. If any check fails, update the playground page NOW — before this task is marked done. This is not optional and does not require a separate request from the user. ``` **`docs/engineering-anti-patterns.md`** — strengthen the EAP-082 entry to include the checklist trigger (find the `## EAP-082` section and append): ```markdown **Trigger (when to run this check):** Any edit to a file matching `src/components/ui/*/` that adds, renames, removes, or redefines a prop or variant. Run the check before composing your response, not after. The check takes 30 seconds; the user asking "did you document this?" wastes a session. **Checklist:** 1. One `ComponentPreview` per variant value in the playground page. 2. `PropsTable` row covers every union member + correct default. 3. `SectionHeading` description names all variants. 4. Flush-and-restart the playground (guardrail-engineering-11) to verify the new preview renders. **History:** ENG-106 occ2 — same feedback, same component category. Promoted to explicit checklist trigger on second recurrence per EAP-008. ``` **`AGENTS.md`** — guardrail #24 already says *ALWAYS update the playground page…* Sharpen it with the trigger condition so it fires at component-edit time, not just at playground-edit time: ```markdown 24. **ALWAYS** update the playground page when adding a new variant, prop, or visual state to any DS component (`src/components/ui/`). **This fires at component-edit time, not playground-edit time.** Before closing any task that touches `src/components/ui/*/`, check `playground/src/app/components//page.tsx` and confirm: (1) one `ComponentPreview` per variant, (2) `PropsTable` covers every union member, (3) description names the variant. See EAP-082. ``` --- ## Principle **Guardrails only enforce what they fire on.** A rule that says "always do X when adding a variant" is inert if it isn't checked at the moment of adding the variant — it becomes a doc to reference rather than a gate to pass. The fix isn't more documentation; it's attaching the check as a **close-out precondition on the component-authoring workflow** so the playground page becomes an exit criterion for every component task, not a follow-up task that gets dropped. This is the pattern from §8.2 of `docs/engineering.md`: when the same category of incident recurs, escalate from background knowledge → soft reference → **hard gate woven into the workflow**. EAP-082 + EAP-008 together name exactly this failure mode.
